title: Prefer 'string.Empty' over an empty string literal
messageFormat: Replace this empty string literal with 'string.Empty'.
summary: An empty string literal "" is easy to confuse with a single space or to overlook entirely in a dense expression. The named member string.Empty states the intent unambiguously.
Reserving string.Empty for the deliberate empty value also makes a genuine literal stand out as carrying real text.
noncompliant: public string Prefix { get; set; } = ""; // Noncompliant
compliant: public string Prefix { get; set; } = string.Empty;
